def: s.m. A bird (in general); a kite; an eagle; a vulture; — an auspicious object or mark, a lucky omen, a prognostic, an omen (in general, but rarely applied to anything inauspicious).
more: s. m. Any lucky or auspicious object or omen. See شگون.

def: s.m. One who understands omens.
s.m. A bird (in general); the Indian kite, Falco cheela; — name of one of the astronomical periods called invariable karaṇs; — name of the Aśvins.
s.m. One who understands omens; — s.f. A hen-sparrow.
s.m. A bird.
s.m. A bird; a blue jay; the Indian vulture.
s.m. A bird; a blue jay; the Indian vulture.
s.m. Name of a famous Sanskrit drama by Kālidās.
s.m. = śakunt, q.v. s.m. A bird; a blue jay; the Indian vulture.
adj. Silent, quiet.

ety: intens. n. fr. سکت; see sākit
def: adj. Constantly or continually silent; — s.m. A silent man.
With family.
s.m. Name of the family, par excellence, of the Buddha (founder of the Buddhist religion); — name of Buddha himself
subs: s.m. 'The Śākya saint'; and śākya-siṅh, s.m. 'The Śākya lion'; epithets of Buddha.

def: adj. Possible, capable, practicable, capable of being effected or done, easy to be accomplished; requiring to be effected or done; that may be conveyed or expressed (as sense or meaning by any particular word); — s.m. (in Gram.) The meaning of a word.
s.f. Possibility, practicability, capability.

s.m. The saul tree, Shorea robusta, and its wood; — the plane-tree; — a wall surrounding a building, a wall (in general); enclosure, court; — a kind of fish, Ophiocephalus wrahl; — the gilt-head fish, Sparus spilotus
more: n. m. S. शाल. 1. (Sant. sarjom ) A common timber tree; Shorea robustā , Wat. 2. (S. शल्य ) Perforation; bore; mortise; a hole made by driving a pin into the ground. 3. (Fr. salle ) A hall; a house; place. 4. A Hindi school. 5. A fish; Ophiocephalus wraht . (?)

n. m. Z. saredha , S. बर्ष A year. sāl-i-āindah , n. m. The next or ensuing year. sāl-i-bāqīdārī . A year of default; a year in which default occurs. sāl ba-sāl , adv. Year by year ( sālāna ). sāl bhar , adv. All the year round. sāl-i-paivastah ; H. tevras , The year before last. sāl tamām, sāl tamāmi , An annual report. sāl tamām par , At the end of the year. sāl jhaṛtī jama kharch . A yearly account of receipts and disbursements; a debit and credit account. sāl hāsil , Yearly produce. sāl-i-hāl, sāl-i-ravāṅ . The present or current year. sāl-i-hisābī, hisābī sāl . The official or financial year. sāl-khurdā , adj. 1. Old; used up; worn out. 2. Experienced. sāl-i-zirāat , Ped. The agricultural year. sāl-i-faslī . The faslī year.

s. m. 1. ( साल ) A common timber tree. (Shorea robusta: Roxb. Pl. Cor. ). 2. ( शल्य ) A thorn. 3. Perforation, bore, mortise, a hole made by driving a pin into the ground, &c. 4. ( शाला ) s. f. House, place. 5. A school. 6. (for श्रृगाल ) s. m. A jackal.

s. m. A year. سال بسال sāl ba-sāl, Year by year, annually. سال حاصل sāl-ḥāṣil, Yearly produce. سال خورده sāl-ḵẖụrda, adj. Old, in years. سالگره sāl-girih, s. f. Birthday, on the anniversary of which a knot is annually added on a string kept for this purpose. سال گشت sāl-gasht, adj. Old, in years. سال وماه sāl o māh, Always, at all times.
subs: s.f. or sāl-pāṇi, s.f. lit. 'Having sāl leaves'; the shrub Hedysarum gangeticum
s.m. A flower of the sāl-tree; — Hibiscus mutabilis
s.m. The resinous juice of the sāl-tree.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian سَال (sāl).

def: s.f. House, place, hall; school-house.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian سَال (sāl).

def: s.m. A thorn; — (fig.) pain, affliction, trouble; — perforation, bore, orifice, mortise; a hole made by driving a pin into the ground, &c.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian سَال (sāl).

def: s.m. A jackal ( = siyār, q.v.). s.m. (f. -ī), A jackal
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian سَال (sāl).

ety: Pehl. śāl; Zend śaredha; S. शरद्
def: s.m. A year
subs: adv. A whole year; all the year round
s.m. & adv. The year before last
s.m. or sāl-tamāmī, s.f. An annual report
adv. At the end of the year
Yearly account of receipts and disbursements; debit and credit account
(or ḥisābī sāl), s.m. Official or financial year
adj. Old in years, old, aged; worn out; — experienced
s.m. Intercalary year; leap year; a year of thirteen months, which comes round every three years

s.f. lit. 'Year-knot, or age-knot'; birthday, anniversary of (one's) birthday (when a knot is tied in a string or thread kept as a record of one's age;-syn. baras-gāṅṭh):-sāl-ě-guẓashta, s.m. The past year, last year
s.m. The mercantile year (concurrent with the Samvat year, but commencing two months later)
adv. By the year; according to the year or years
adv. lit. 'Years and months'; always, at all times.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian سَال (sāl).

def: s.m. (dialec.) The large green grasshopper; the mantis or leaf-insect.
s.m. Stake, spike, splinter, thorn; difficulty, embarrassment, &c. (see śalya)
subs: (apnā), To meet or manage a difficulty, &c., to manage difficult affairs (of a house, &c.); to prepare (one's own) funeral pyre, to become a satī
v.n. To be clear or free of difficulty or embarrassment, to be settled or composed.

def: adj. (colloq.) One hundred ( = sau).
s.m. Water ( = sara or sar).
s.m. The tree Shorea robusta ( = sāl); — a kind of fish, Ophiocephalus wrahl; — a name of king Śāli-vāhan
subs: or śāl-bhanjī, s.f. A doll, a wooden puppet; a kind of game played in the east of India
s.f. lit. 'Having śāl leaves'; the shrub Hedysarum gangeticum
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian شال (šāl). Doublet of سَاڑِی (sāṛī). Compare Punjabi ਸ਼ਾਲ (śāl) / شال (śāl), Gujarati શાલ (śāl), Marathi शाल (śāl), Kannada ಶಾಲು (śālu), Telugu శాలువ (śāluva).

def: s.m. A porcupine's quill; — a dart, spear: — a kind of field; — an epithet of Brahmā.
more: (Heb. To plunder, to spoil) adj. One who can take nothing in his hands (from sickness), paralytick, having the hand luxated or withered.

def: v. a. 1. To prepare one's own funeral pyre; to become a satī , q. v. Tiryā tujh meṅ tīn gun, aur augan haiṅ lakh chār, Mangal gāve, sal rache, aur kokan upjeṅ lāl . Three virtues, woman, yours, in faults four hundred thousand set, You benisons sing, self immolate, and children you beget. 2. To manage the house. sal se baiṭhnā , v. n. To be settled or composed.
s.f. House, place (used chiefly in comp., e.g. dharm-sālā, pāṭ-sālā, qq.v., and cf. sāl).
more: n. m. S. श्याल 1. A wife's brother. 1. Jitne kāle, utne mere bāp ke sāle . Prov. As many blacks so many brothers-in-law my father has had. 2. Rāvan kā sālā . Prov. The brother-in-law of the great Rāvan. (Said of one who tyrannizes under the protection of a powerful person). 2. A common term of abuse implying the disgrace attaching to a man whose sister has been had by another; hence, a pander; pimp. Ranḍī māṅge rupaiyā—'Le le, merī Maiyā, Phakkaṛ māṅge paisā—'Chal be, sāle, kaisā ?' Prov. The harlot money doth demand—'Take take it, mammy dear, it's here,' The beggar wants a pice—'Out pander of thy sister's honor dear!' jagat-sālā , n. m. 1. A brother of a harlot. 2. An abuse.
s. m. 1. (for श्याल ) A wifeʼs brother, brother-in-law. 2. ( शाला in compos. mostly) House, place.

def: s.m. Wife's brother, brother-in-law; a term of abuse.
s.f. A large branch of a tree; — a hall, room, house, place; a school, a stable ( = sālā).
s.f. Calling or inviting (beggars, &c.), to receive or partake of food; invitation; annunciation; proclamation; voice, call, cry (of an auctioneer, or salesman, &c.); shout, challenge (of a foe).
more: n. m. An invitation. Salā na shud, balā shud . P. Prov. No feast of pleasure, of ills a measure.

def: (fr. sāl), adj. aff. Years old, &c. (used with numerals, e.g. du-sāla, 'Two years old'; ṣad-sāla, 'of a hundred years').
more: ( In comp .) A termination affixed to numbers, as do-sālah , two year old.
(in compos.) A termination affixed to numbers, to denote so many years old; as دوساله do-sāla, Two years old; پنجساله panj-sāla, Five years old; صد ساله ṣad-sāla, Of a hundred years.

def: s.m. A fox
subs: s.f. Salep, the root of a species of orchis, Orchis mascula (considered, particularly in the East, to be a strong restorative and aphrodisiac).

def: s.m. A (hard, rough) stone or slab for grinding drugs, &c. on (syn. kharal).

ety: for A. صلابة, inf. n. of صلب 'to be hard,' &c.
def: s.f. Firmness, hardness, stiffness, rigidity; severity, rigour; strength; valour; majesty, dignity, awe.
more: (from صلب) s. f. Firmness, hardness, severity, majesty, dignity.

def: s. (dakh.) An intrenchment ( Harris ).
s.m. = شلبهہ śalabh, q.v.
s.m. A grasshopper; a locust.
salad
wety: Borrowed from Portuguese salada or English salad.

ety: inf. n. of سلف 'to pass away,' &c.
def: adj. & s.m. Past, preceding, former (times, &c.); — such as have gone before, predecessors, ancestors; past or former times; — money advanced for merchandise, &c.; — a purse, bag.
more: n. f. سلف passed away. Past or old times. Salaf se hotī āī hai .

(from سلف To level the ground, to go before) 1. adj. Past, preceding, former (times). 2. s. m. Ancestors. 3. Money advanced for merchandise, &c. 4. A purse, a bag. 5. The past, former times. ¶ silf, Brother-in-law, husband of a wifeʼs sister.
s. (dakh.) A barge, a bark ( Harris ).

def: adj. Joined together, in one piece, unbroken, continuous, uninterrupted; — s.m. (local), Close friendship, intimacy, familiarity.
more: adj. Unbroken; uninterrupted; continuous. Ek salag paṭṭī utār lo .

def: v. n. (dakh.) See سلگنا sulagnā.

s.m. A species of black quartzose (found in the river Gandhak), containing the impression of one or more ammonites (conceived by the Hindūs to represent Vishṇu; and hence it is worshipped by the Vaishṇavas).
more: n. m. S. शालग्राम An ovoid black quartzose worn by the action of water and sometimes found impregnated with gold. It bears the impression of one or more ammonites, conceived by the Hindus to represent Vishnū who was turned to stone by a curse of Binda, wife of the Rakshas or demon Jalandar. Kūṛh nār se murgī bhalī jo ande deve bīs, Sālagrām se chakkī bhalī jo āṭā deve pīs . Kabīr. For a stupid wife give me a hen that twenty eggs will lay, For holy stone give me a mill to grind my corn each day.
s. m. A flinty stone, containing the impression of one or more ammonites, conceived by the Hindūs to represent Vish̤ṇu.

ety: inf. n. of صلح 'to be good, right, just, &c.
def: s.f. Goodness (of character, or of state or condition); well-being, prosperity; rectitude, virtue, probity, honesty; right or proper course (of conduct or procedure); good and proper thing, right thing, prudent measure; advisableness, propriety, fitness; advice, counsel; consultation; — (quasi-inf. n. of اصلح, iv of صلح), rectification, adjustment, agreement, harmony, concord, reconciliation, peace, treaty ( = iṣlāḥ, q.v.); — adj. Good, right, proper, suitable, favourable, advisable, prudent
more: n. f. صلح was right. 1. Counsel (اپديش 1, 3, & islāh ). Nek salāh kā pūchhnā kyā? Prov. Saying. No one refuses a good thing. 2. Choice; volition ( irādah , 1-4). Ṭhairī hai unke āne kī yān kal pe jā salāh, Aë jān-i-barlab-āmdah, hai is meṅ terī kyā salāh! Zauq. To-morrow she 'll here, 'tis settled now at last, What say you to it, Life! from me now ebbing fast? salāh par chalnā , v. a. To act according to advice. salāh denā , v. a. To advise; counsel. salāh se , adv. In communication with; with the advice of. salāh-kār, salāh dene-vālā , n. m. An adviser; a counsellor. salāh-kār-i-ryāsat ; H. rāj-mantrī , n. m. A counsellor of State. salāh k., yā lenā , v. a. To consult; advise with; take counsel. salāh nahīṅ . Not advisable; inexpedient. salāh-i-vaqt , adj. Advisable. salāh o mashvarah , n. m. Advice and counsel. be-salāh k. , v. a. To act without advice. pakkī salāh . 1. Sound policy. 2…

(from صلح Heb. To prosper) 1. s. f. Peace, concord, treaty. 2. Rectitude, integrity, goodness of state or condition, prosperity. 3. Advice, counsel. 4. adj. Advisable. صلاح کرني ṣalāḥ karnī, To consult, advise, to take counsel together.
subs: (kisi-kī), To act according to the advice (of)
(-ko), To give advice (to), to advise, counsel
(-se), To hold consultation (with), to advise (with), to consult
(-kī), To take the advice (of); to consult
Is not advisable; is inexpedient; is not a prudent course
s.f. A fit or proper opportunity, a favourable time; — the right course for the time or occasion; — adj. Advisable.
wety: Borrowed from Arabic صَلَاح (ṣalāḥ).

ety: for A. صلاحية, inf. n. of صلح 'to be good,' &c.
def: s.f. Goodness, rectitude, integrity, probity, honesty; virtue, chastity; sanctity; — welfare, well-being, prosperity; — mildness, gentleness; — ability, capability, fitness, worth; — a deposition; a report or return (made by the police) on the state of a district
more: n. f. 1. Capacity; capability. 2. Mildness; gentleness. 3. A deposition ( izhār , 3). 4. A report or return made by the police. salāhiyāt-bahī , n. f. A diary kept in police and revenue offices. salāhiyat likhnā , v. n. To keep a register of travellers in a road-side inn.
(from صلح) s. f. Virtue, chastity, integrity, goodness, probity, sanctity.
subs: s.f. A diary (kept in police and revenue offices) of the condition or prosperity of a district
(-kī), To make a (police) report on the state of a district; to keep a register of travellers in a sarāʼc, &c.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian صَلَاحِیَّت (salāhiyyat), from Arabic صَلَاحِيَّة (ṣalāḥiyya). By surface analysis, صَلاح (salāh) + ـِیَت (-iyat).

def: (from سلاح Armour, and شور Exercise, habitude) adj. A champion, gladiator, horse-breaker.

s.f. A piece of wire, a needle, a probe, any pointed surgical instrument; a catheter; a bodkin; a large needle used for tinging the eyes with collyrium; a piece of wire, or needle, &c. used to deprive a person of sight (by drawing it, while hot, over the eyes); a lead-pencil; a brimstone match; a line, streak, stripe
more: n. f. S. शलाका A. salābah . 1. Any thing in the form of a needle. 2. A large needle used for tinging the eyelids with a collyrium. 3. A large needle, probe, or wire heated and drawn across the eyes so as to cause loss of sight. 4. A bodkin. 5. A catheter. 6. (Fac.) Membrum virile . 7. Matches ( dīyā-salāī ). 8. A line; stripe. salāī phernā , v. a. To blind; deprive of sight. salāī-dār , adj. Striped or twilled (cloth).
subs: (-par), To pass a red-hot wire or needle, &c. (across the eyes of a person), to deprive of sight, to blind

def: s.f. Brother-in-law's wife ( = sarhaj).
more: Māṛ. n. f. S. श्याल जाया Brother-in-law's wife.

def: s.f. = sarhaj and salaij, qq.v. s.f. (dialec.), Wife's brother's wife (cf. sāṛhū).
plural of سلجوقی (saljūqī)
wety: Borrowed from Arabic سَلَاجِقَة (salājiqa).

def: n. f. S. शिलाजतु 1. Storax; stone lac; mineral coal; bitumen; red chalk; benzoin. 2. A beautiful crystallized foliated gypsum.
adj. (f. -ā), Feeling shame or modesty, bashful, modest, ashamed.
more: ( स With, and लज्जा Shame) adj. Shameful, ashamed.

def: adj. Painful, afflictive, troublous.
s.f. (local) = salāḵẖ, q.v.; and cf. next. s.f. A bar (of iron); an ingot (of gold or silver); a stake; a spit; a probe; — a continuous line, a streak.
s.f. A probe; a rule, ruler, &c. (see śalākā); a line, streak.
more: s. f. 1. A rule, a ruler. 2. A probe.

def: s.f. A dart, a javelin; an arrow; a rib (of an umbrella, &c.); a thin bar (of a cage, &c.); a small stake, stick, rod, peg, pin; a round pointed piece of wood or bamboo (serving for various purposes); a rule, ruler; a brimstone-match; any pointed surgical instrument, a tent or probe; a bone; an oblong quadrangular piece of ivory or bone (used in particular games), a domino; a fibrous stick (used as a brush, &c.); a tooth-brush; a toothpick; a pencil; — a porcupine; — a kind of bird, Turdus salica.
s.f. A bar (of iron); an ingot (of gold or silver); a stake; a spit; a probe; — a continuous line, a streak.
more: n. m. S. शलाका 1. An iron bar. 2. An ingot (of gold or silver). 3. Streak; line. Sharāb kī salākh bandh gaī .
wety: From Sanskrit शलाका (śalākā).

def: v.t. To rule lines; to mark with lines, to streak.
s.m. = S شللي शलली śalalī, s.f. A porcupine's quill.
s.f. = S شلل शलल śalal, s.m. A porcupine's quill.

ety: inf. n. of سلم 'to be or become safe,' &c.
def: s.m. Safety, peace; salutation, greeting, compliments; parting salutation, adieu, farewell, good-bye
more: n. m. سلم was safe. 1. Blessing ( darūd ). 2. Salutation; compliments. Maiṅ to tum ko dūr hī se salām kartā hūṅ . I always salute you from a distance. (I keep my distance). 3. Adieu; farewell; goodbye. Aisī dostī ko salām hai! Adieu to such friendship! salām-alaikum, salām-alaik , Mah. n. f. lit . Peace be with you! Good morning. Mil gaë kī salām-alaik hai . Prov. Chance meetings and bowing acquaintances. salām k. , v. a. 1. To salute; greet; hail. 2. To bid adieu; say farewell. Jab se to sang nehā kīnā, sunā na sukh kā nām, Pītam terī pīt ko jhuk jhuk karūṅ salām! Since I did love you even in name no peace I've had with you, My husband! to such love as yours I humbly bid adieu. 3. To bow to or acknowledge one's superiority. Akelī jāo jo masjid meṅ tāq bharne ko Dugānā jān, tumheṅ jhuk-ke ham salām kareṅ! Jan Sāhab. salām phernā , Mah. v. n. To look to the right and then to the left afte…

(from سلم) s. m. 1. Salutation. 2. Peace, safety. سلام کرنا salām karnā, 1. To salute, to hail, to accost. 2. To give up, to desist, to refrain, to bid adieu. سلامٌ عليک salāmuṉ aﻌlaika, Peace or safety to thee: سلامٌ عليکم salāmuṉ aﻌlaikum (Heb. ) Peace, &c. to you (forms of salutation). سلام لينا salām lenā, To return the salutation.
subs: To look to the right and the left after prayer
(-ko), To bid adieu (to), to dismiss; (among Europeans) to present (one's) compliments (to); to request the presence (of a person); to desire permission to enter
Peace be with you; good morning to you
(-ko), To salute, greet, hail, accost; to bid adieu (to), say farewell (to); to give up, to desist or refrain (from); to bow (to), to acknowledge the superiority of
(-kā), To accept and return the salutation (of)
ex: Send my regards to your father.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian سَلَام (salām), itself from Arabic سَلَام (salām). First attested in c. 1503 as Middle Hindi سلام (slam).

ety: inf. n. of سلم 'to be safe,' &c.
def: s.m. Saluting; submitting; cultivating peace; friendship; — peace, safety; — adj. Free from fault or flaw, sound; — sallamahuʼl-lāhǒ taʻālā, May the Most High God save or protect him.
more: (from سلم Heb. To be safe, entire or sound) 1. s. m. Peace, safety. 2. Saluting, submitting, cultivating peace, friendship. 3. adj. Free from fault or flaw. ¶ sullam, s. m. A stair. سلمه الله تعاليٰ sallamahu-l-lāhu taʼāﻌlạ̄, May the Almighty protect him.

def: salaam alaikum (Muslim greeting)
wety: From the Arabic سَلَامٌ عَلَيْكُم (salāmun ʕalaykum, “peace be upon you”).

def: a hi, hello (term for a mere brief exchange of pleasantries as opposed to a longer discussion with a person)
wety: From سَلام (salām, “hello; peace”) + دُعا (du'ā, “prayer; supplication”).

r: ṣalʻam, ṣallaʼllāh ʻalaihi wa sallam, ṣallạ̄
def: a contrac. (often added to the name of Mohammad), formed from the abbreviation of the words ṣallaʼllāh ʻalaihi wa sallam, 'May God bless him and grant him peace' (see صلی ṣallạ̄).
more: A mysterious word (added often to the name of Maḥammad ) formed from the abbreviation of the following words, صلي الله عليه وسلم ṣallạ-l-lāh aﻌlaihi wa sallam, The blessing and peace of God be with him.

ety: for A. سلامة, inf. n. of سلم; see salām
def: s.f. Safety, salvation; tranquillity, peace, rest, repose; immunity; liberty; soundness; recovery; health; — adj. & adv. (used predicatively) Safe, sound, well; — in safety, safely, securely
more: adj. Safe; well. Khudā salāmat rakkhe! Benediction. Peace be with you! salāmat , adv. Safely; securely. 1. Salāmat pahuṅchā . 2. Nāk kuṭī mubārak; sir muṅḍā salāmat! Wom. Nose-cut, good fortune; head-shaven, hail! (A shameless woman.)
(from سلم) s. f. Safety, salvation, recovery, tranquillity, health; adjectively, Safe, well, in safety. سلامت رو salāmat-rau, An economist. سلامت روي salāmat-rau-ī, s. f. Economy, good management.
subs: s.m. An economist; a good manager or administrator; a well-conducted person
or rauʼī, s.f. Economy, good management; moderation; good behaviour
v.n. To be or remain safe and sound, to live long, to prosper.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian سَلَامَت (salāmat), from Arabic سَلَامَة (salāma).

def: bless you, stay safe and healthy
wety: سلامت (salāmat, “health”) + باشد (bāśad, “be”).

def: bless you, stay safe and healthy
wety: سلامت (salāmat, “health”) + باشید (bāśed, “be”).

def: s.f. Safety, security, protection; peace, tranquillity; health; good condition, well-being; the being alive and in health; — a kind of coarse cloth; — adv. In peace or safety; in health
more: n. f. 1. Protection; peace (امن 1.). Allah miyāṅ kī salāmtī paṛh-kar nyāz de do! Mah. Wom. 2. Health; good condition; life. Tumhārī salāmtī meṅ aisī bāteṅ hoṅgī. salāmtī se , Mah. Wom. intj. Heaven preserve! Uske to salamtī se do bachche hain . salāmtī kā jām pīnā , v. a. To drink to one's health.
s. f. 1. Health, safety; in health or safety. 2. A coarse kind of cloth.
subs: (-kī), To drink to the health (of), to quaff a cup to the health (of).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian سَلَامَتِی (salāmatī).

def: n. m. 1. An economist; a good manager or administrator. 2. A well-conducted person.
n. f. 1. Economy; good management; moderation. 2. Good behaviour. salāmat rahnā , v. n. To be safe and sound; to live long; prosper.

ety: salām+ī = Zend aff. i = S. इ
def: s.f. Salutation; salute (as by presentation of arms, &c.), salute of cannon; — a present on being introduced to a superior; a present given to a landlord on granting a lease, &c.; a present (on particular occasions) to a person making a salute; a congratulatory present to a bridegroom (generally from the father-in-law's household; — cf. salām-karāʼī); — slope, incline; a pavement across the bed of a watercourse
more: n. f. 1. Salutation; presentation of arms; reception; welcome. Bādshāh kī salāmī ke vāste fauj khaṛī hai . 2. A salute of guns; salvo of artillery. Yeh topeṅ kisī Rājā kī salāmī meṅ dag rahī haīṅ . 3. A present to a superior, or to a bridegroom. It is given by the tenant to a landlord for granting him a lease of land, etc. or by one receiving an appointment, or paying a visit to a great man. 4. A slope ( ḍhalān ). Saṛak kī salāmī . 5. A pavement across the bed of a water-course; an Irish bridge. salāmī khānah-bārī , n. A compulsory present or tax exacted by the zamīndār from his tenant on his erecting a new hut.
s. f. 1. Salute. 2. A present given to a landlord for granting a lease, &c.
s. f. A present given on particular occasions to a person making a salute.
subs: s.f. A present from a farmer to his landlord to enable him to build his house; a compulsory present exacted by the landlord from a tenant on his erecting a new hut.

def: s.m. A curry of meat, fish, or vegetables.
more: n. m. H. salonā salted. Meat, fish, or vegetable curry.
s. m. Meat, or fish, &c. eaten with bread or rice.
s. m. Meat, fish, or vegetables dressed with condiments in various ways, a curry.
wety: Uncertain. Some theories: * Inherited from Sanskrit *संलेपन (*saṃlepana, “smearing together”), from सम्- (sam-) + लिप् (lip, “to smear”) + -न (-na). * Inherited from Sanskrit *संलापन (*saṃlāpana), from the root ली (lī, “to attach”). * Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀲𑀮𑁄𑀡 (saloṇa), 𑀲𑀮𑀽𑀡 (salūṇa), from Sanskrit सलवण (salavaṇa, “having salt”), from स- (sa-) + लवण (lavaṇa). Compare सलोना (salonā, “tasty”).

ety: sāl, q.v.+Zend aff. āna or ana = S. अन
def: adj. & adv. Yearly, annual; annually, per year; — s.m. Annual pension; annuity; — land taken up for the whole year
more: adj. Annual; yearly. sālānah , n. m. 1. Land taken up for the whole year. 2. Annual pension; an annuity. sālānahāmdanī , n. f. Annual income or revenue. sālānah hāsil, yā tahsīl . Yearly collections from a district or estate. sālānah hīn hayāt , n. A life-annuity. sālānah-dār , An annuitant; a pensioner. naqshajāt-i-sālānah . Annual returns.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian سالانه (sālāna).

def: adj. Penetrable.
s.f. coutrac. of salūno, q.v.

ety: sāl, q.v.+ār = āwar = prep. ā+war = Zend ā+bara = S. आ+भर
def: s.m. Leader in war, general or commander-in-chief, a title given by Eastern princes to their nobles
more: n. m. In comp . A chief; head; sālār jaṅg , n. m. 1. A general or commander-in-chief. 2. ( Slang ) A brother-in-law. sālār-i-qāfla . The chief or leader of a caravan. sālār-i-qaum , n. m. The head of a clan or seet.
s. m. A chief, head, leader, general or commander (of an army), prince, captain. سالارقافله sālār-i-ḳāfila, The chief of a caravan, leader of a troop.
subs: s.m. Chief of a caravan, leader of a troop
s.m. Head of a clan or tribe.

adj. (prop. fem. of s̤alās̤at), Three.

def: s.f. Easiness, simplicity; plainness, clearness, perspicuity; facility; ductility; gentleness, tractableness; urbanity.
more: n. f. سلس was loose. 1. Simplicity. 2. Gentleness; urbanity. salāsat-i-zabān , n. f. Simplicity of language.
(from سلس) s. f. Perspicuity, clearness, plainness, facility, ductility, easiness, gentleness.

ety: v.n. fr. صلب 'to be hard'; 'to crucify'

more: n. f. A cross or crucifix. salīb denā, salīb par chaṛhānā , v. a. To crucify.

ety: for A. سليقة salīqat, v.n. fr. سلق 'to adapt or dispose to,' &c.
def: s.m. Nature, natural disposition or constitution; genius; taste; good disposition; method, knack, way; knowledge, skill, dexterity, address
more: n. m. See tamīz . Knack ( ḍhaṅg , 1). 1. Salīqah bāt kā yeh, kuchh zabāṅ jab apnī khole hai , To apne vālid-i-marhūm ko mahrūm bole hai . Sauda. His skill in language such that when to speak he opes his mouth, His father blessed in Heaven he calls his father damned in sooth. 2. Shart salīqa hai har kām meṅ . Prov. In every thing you do good judgment helps you through. salīqa-mand, salīqa-shiār, salīqah-dār, sāhib-i-salīqah, salīqa-vālā, salīqevālī , adj. See tamīz-dār . salīqa kī guftgū , n. m. Polite conversation. salīqa-i-majlis , n. m. Good breeding.

s. m. Nature, genius, taste, good disposition, method, knack, knowledge, dexterity, address. سليقه شعار salīḳa-shiʼāﻌr, adj. Of good disposition. سليقه گفتگو salīḳa-e-guft-gū, s. m. Polite conversation. صاحب سليقه ṣāḥib-salīḳa, A man of taste. سليقه مجلس salīḳa-e-majlis, s. m. Good breeding.
subs: salīqa-shěʻār, salīqa-mand, and salīqa-wālā, adj. Of good disposition; well-conducted; possessed of genius; discerning, discreet
s.m. or salīqe-kī guftgū, s.f. Polite conversation

def: n. m. The year of 13 months, which comes round every three years. sāl-girah ; H. baras-gāṅṭh , n. m. Hin. sāl-i-guzashtā ; Sant. dinkalom , The past year; last year. sāl-i-mālī , n. m. The revenue year. sāl-i-mahājnī . Accounts of the annual collections and balances for a series of years. sālhā sāl , adv. For years; year after year. ab-ke sāl, is sāl , n. The present or current year.
s.m. Water; liquid (in general).

def: s.f. Creeping, crawling; sound as of a snake creeping, rustling; creeping sensation, titillation, irritation (caused by the crawling of an insect on the body), formication; itching; prurience; pricking, piercing; uneasy feeling, doubt; rankling (cf. sursurāhaṭ).
n. f. A creeping, tickling, or itching sensation. Jab zakhm achchhā hone pe ātā hai, to pahle salsalāhaṭ hotī hai .

ety: prob. S. शल् redupl.+ā = āw = Prk. āwe = S. āpi (caus. augment); cf. sarsarānā
def: v.n. To creep, crawl; to feel a creeping or tickling sensation; to itch; to prick, pierce; to be troubled with doubt; to be uneasy; to rankle (in); — to make a rustling sound (as the creeping of a snake), to rustle; — v.t. To cause to simmer or boil, &c., to heat.
more: v. n. 1. ( rauryānā ) To feel a creeping sensation (from an ant, etc.). 2. To tickle; titillate. 3. To itch. 1. Phunsī salsalā rahī hai . 2. Hathelī salsalā rahī hai, kahīṅ se rupayā āëgā .
v. n. 1. To creep (as, a snake), &c. See سرسرانا sarsarānā. 2. (dakh.) To rankle: see کهٿکنا.

def: s.f. contrac. of salsalāhaṭ, q.v.

(˚ya+ud˚), s.m. The extraction of extraneous substances (as arrows, splinters, thorns, &c.) from the body; the ceremony of cleansing the site of a new house from bones or other unclean substances; the ceremony of cleansing or purifying a new house.
ex: ہمارے کشور کے لیے زیادہ سے زیادہ راجہ شلیہ جیسے مرد ہونے چاہئیں ہے
hamāre kiśvar ke liye zyāda se zyāda rāja śalya jaise mard hone cāh'ī̃ he
let there be many men like king Shalya for our fatherland
کس کی فرہ ہمارے شہنشاہ شلیہ سے بہترین ہے
kis kī farrah hamāre śehanśāh śalya se behtarīn hai?
whose glory is greater than our king of kings, Shalya?
wety: Learned borrowing from Sanskrit शल्य (śalya, “spear; javelin; arrow”). Distantly related to Ancient Greek κῆλον (kêlon, “shafts (of arrows)”), Old Prussian kelian (“spear”), Old Norse hali, Albanian thel.

wety: Inherited from Middle Hindi سماں (smaṉ) (c. 1780), from Prakrit 𑀲𑀫𑀅 (samaa), from Sanskrit स॒म॒य (samayá). Doublet of سَمَے (samai).

def: s.m. Time, season; season of plenty, season of greatest abundance or prevalence; plenty, abundance; opportunity; — state, condition; concord, harmony, unison; — scene, landscape, view
more: n. m. S. समय 1. Time; season. 1. Rājā ke darbār meṅ jaiye samayo pāë, Sāīṅ, tahāṅ na baiṭhye, jahāṅ kou de uṭhāë! Jahāṅ koī dei uṭhāë bol an-bole rahiye! Haṅsye, nāh hirāë, bāt pūchhe teṅ kahiye! Kaheṅ Girdhar Kabirāë, samaë soṅ kīje kājā! Atī ātur nahīṅ hoë, bahur ankhe hai rājā . Girdhar. When chance shall serve that you a royal court essay, Sit not, where one may say 'go lower down, I pray!' And wheresoe'er you 're placed keep silence nor aught say, Nor laugh, nor speak, but when you 're spoken to you may, Says Girdhar, 'let each act upon occasion stay,' 'Quick be not lest the king to you rebuke convey!' 2. Kaheṅ Girdhar, Kabirāë, tanik nahīṅ kīnā ros'ā, Same paṛe kī bāt, bilār ko sīkh det hai mūsā . Girdhar Kunḍalya. The Poet Girdhar says, 'take not, I pray, my words amiss, As times shall go the mouse with counsel shall the cat dismiss.' 3. Samā kare, nar kyā kare, sameṅ sameṅ kī bāt…
s. m. 1. Time, season. 2. Plenty, abundance. 3. State, condition. 4. Concord, harmony, unison. سما بندهنا samā bandhnā, v. n. To be in concert.
(Heb. ) s. m. 1. The heavens, the sky, the firmament. 2. A canopy. 3. Altitude.
subs: (-kā), To make a picture or representation (of), to represent; — to act, sing, or play in concert, to produce harmony; to be in concert or harmony

ety: for A. سماجة, inf. n. of سمج 'to be foul, unseemly, or ugly'

def: n. f. S. सम + बुध 1. See budh , 1, 2. 1. Apnī apnī samajh kā pher hai .Prov.Saying. Many men of many minds. ( Sot homines, tot sententiœ ). 2. Tumhārī samajh par to patthar paṛ gaë . A stone hath fallen on your good sense and crushed it. (You have gone coon). 2. Judgment; apprehension; opinion; view. samajh ānā , v. n. 1. To grow to years of discretion. Thoṛī thoṛī mohe samajh jo āī, ek jā thairī morī sagāī, Āvan lāge bāman, nāī, koī le rupaiyā, koī le dhelīt Girls' song. 2. To get sense; return to one's senses. samajh būjh . Understanding; comprehension. samajh būjh-kar razāmandī , G. G. Intelligent consent. samajh-dār, samajhne-vālā ; Illit. samajhvār , adj. Sensible; wise; prudent. Samajhne-vāle kī maut hai . Prov. Saying. Knowledge is death. (Ignorance is bliss). samajh meṅ ānā , v. n. To understand; comprehend. achchhī samajh . A good understanding. ulṭī samajh . A crooked understanding.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀲𑀁𑀩𑀼𑀚𑁆𑀛 (saṃbujjha). Compare also سمجھنا (samajhnā).

def: s.f. Knowledge, perception, understanding, comprehension, discernment, conception, thought, mind, sense, judgment, opinion, view
more: (perhaps, from सम् Together, and ऊत्द्य from r. ऊह Reason) s. f. Understanding, comprehension, knowledge, opinion, conception, thought, mind.
subs: (kisī-ko or mujh-ko), To get sense or understanding, to attain to years of discretion; to recover (one's) senses
adj. Discerning, intelligent, sensible, wise, prudent
(-kī, or-merī, &c.), 'To come into the comprehension' (of), to be comprehended, be understood, be perceived, be apprehended.

ety: samujh˚ or samajh˚ = Prk. संवुजझ(इ) = S. सम्बुध्य(ते), rt. सम्+बुध्
def: v.t. & v.n. To perceive, know, understand, comprehend, apprehend; to learn; — to think, consider, conceive, suppose, deem, imagine, fancy; to think highly of, to consider as good or excellent or remarkable; — to come to an understanding (with, -se), to look (to one, for explanation, or payment, &c.), to settle accounts (with, either fig. or lit.), to be even (with)
more: v. n. 1. See būjhnā , 1, 3. 1. Huā so huā, ab to samjho . 2. Kuchh tum samjhe, kuchh ham samjhe . Prov. You think one way, and I another. ☞ A foot passenger, carrying some treasure, asked a horseman to relieve him of his load as far as the next stage. The horseman declined to burden himself, and went on. Soon after the horseman regretted that he had missed such a rare opportunity of making off with so much money, while the foot pad, on his part, congratulated himself on the other's refusal, but for which he might have lost his money. The two met again, and, on the horseman offering to take charge of the treasure the other replied in the words of the Proverb. 2. To look to one (for payment, etc.). Chunarī kī rangāī more pīyā se samajh lījo . Wom. 3. To retaliate; to be even with. samajh-ke , adv. Knowingly; deliberately. samajh-ke k. , v. a. To act intelligently; to exercise discretion. b…
(see سمجهہ samajh ) v. a. To understand, comprehend, suppose, think, conceive, consider, deem, fancy, perceive, apprehend, know, come to an understanding (with).
subs: (-ko), and samajh-lenā, v.t. intens. of and = samajhnā.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀲𑀁𑀩𑀼𑀚𑁆𑀛𑀤𑀺 (saṃbujjhadi), from Sanskrit सम्बुध्यते (sambudhyate). Cognate with Punjabi ਸਮਝਣਾ (samjhaṇā) / سَمجھݨا (samjhṇā), Gujarati સમજવું (samajvũ), Marathi समजणे (samajṇe).

Furniture, baggage, articles, things, paraphernalia; requisites, necessaries, materials, appliances; instrument, tools, apparatus; provision made for any necessary occasion, necessary preparations; pomp, circumstance; — measure, quantity, proportion; order, arrangement, disposition; mode; custom, habit; power, strength; probity; opulence; understanding, reason, intellect; — boundary, limit; landmark; — sāmān-ě-balāʼe, s.m. Household furniture (such as beds, boxes, pots and pans, &c.)
more: n. m. 1. Things (اسباب 2—4, 7). 2. Preparations; arrangements. Lomṛī ke shikār ko sher kā sāmān chāhiye . Prov. To hunt a fox you need the appliances for killing a tiger. (Even a small work needs large appliances). 3. Sign; token. sāmān-i-jaṅg, yā harb , n. m. The materials for war. be-sar o sāmāṅ , adj. Poor; indigent ( be-chārah ).

adj. S. समान 1. Common; general. 2. Like ( tul ). sāmān bhūt , ( Gram .) The past or perfect tense. sāmānya-lakshan , n. m. A generic character. sāmān yā samān meṅ ānā , Wom. v. n. 1. To be appeased, pacified. Aisī bigṛī ki abtak sāmān meṅ nahīn āī . Wom. 2. To come to one's senses. Zarā sāmān meṅ-ā-kar bāt karo!

s. m. f. 1. Furniture, apparatus, necessaries, instruments, tools. 2. Measure, quantity. 3. Understanding, reason, intellect. 4. Custom, habit, mode, order, proportion. 5. Opulence, power. 6. Probity. 7. A landmark. سامان جنگي sāmān-i-jangī, s. m. War-like apparatus, ammunition. بي سروسامان be sar-o-sāmān, adj. 1. Destitute, indigent, helpless. 2. Stupified, brainless. 3. Immense, boundless, endless (applied to affairs).
subs: adj. Destitute, indigent, helpless; immense, boundless, endless; stupefied, brainless.

ety: samā˚ = samāw˚ = Prk. समाव(इ or सम्+आप्
def: v.n. To be contained or held (in, -meṅ), to go or get (in or into), to enter (in or into), to fit (in); to take up room fill or occupy space.
more: v. n. सं + मा 1. To be contained in; to fit; go into. Naṭ chaṛhe, bāṅs baṛhe; naṭ utre ghaṭ jāë, E sakhī, maiṅ to se pūckhūṅ naṭ meṅ bāṅs samāë? Riddle. Ans. Makṛī. Spider. The clown! the pole! It grows and shrinks as he goes up and down: Can you explain it dear? Methinks it's packed inside the clown. 2. Jahāṅ jiske sīng samāëṅ vahāṅ chale jāo . Where'er your horn fits in there go! (Live where you can). 3. Kājal ḍālūṅ kirkirā, aur surmā dīya na jāë, In nainan meṅ pī base, dūjā kaun samāë? Wom. Lamp-black doth prick my eyes, for eye-paint there's no space, My love dwells in my eyes, for aught else there's no place. 4. Zamīn phat jāë to maiṅ samā jāoṅ! Would the earth but open that I the gap might fill! 2. To fill, or occupy space. 1. Ek samānā sakal meṅ, sakal samāṅā tāhī . God dwelleth in all things and all things dwell in Him. 2. Sūrat mere mitr kī man meṅ rahī samāë, Joṅ meṅhdī ke pāt …
To be contained in, to go into. See امانا amānā.

def: s.m. The being in the same predicament; common office or function or government; the condition of relating to the same object.
